title: Engineering
intro: Questions on the Engineering unit form a small but varied canon spanning several subfields rather than clustering around any single dominant topic. Bridges are the single most frequent answerline, typically keyed to structural design types like beam, arch, suspension, and cable-stayed construction, alongside the general concept of structural stability. Materials and manufacturing make up a second cluster, covering both traditional construction material like concrete and modern fabrication methods like extrusion and 3D printing.

The remaining answerlines are drawn from mechanical and electrical engineering fundamentals: pistons and screws represent basic mechanical components for converting motion and force, frequency represents the core electrical concept underlying circuit and signal analysis, and wind turbines represent renewable energy generation. Taken together, the unit samples broadly across engineering's major branches rather than probing any one of them in depth.

sections.name: Civil & Structural Engineering
sections.blurb: Bridges, buildings, and the structural principles that keep them standing.

entries.note: Structures spanning gaps like valleys or waterways, classified by design (beam, arch, suspension, cable-stayed) and analyzed for load-bearing capacity.
Property of a structure or system to return to equilibrium after a disturbance, a key consideration in structural design.

sections.name: Mechanical Engineering
sections.blurb: Machines and the mechanical components that convert and transmit force and motion.

entries.note: Cylindrical components that move within a cylinder to convert pressure into linear motion, central to internal combustion engines.
Simple machines with a helical ridge, or thread, that convert rotational motion into linear force, used as fasteners.

sections.name: Materials & Manufacturing
sections.blurb: Construction materials and the processes used to shape and fabricate them.

entries.note: Additive manufacturing devices that build objects layer by layer from digital models, using materials like plastic filament or resin.
Composite construction material of cement, aggregate, and water, reinforced with steel rebar for added tensile strength.
Manufacturing process forcing material through a shaped die to create objects with a fixed cross-sectional profile.

sections.name: Electrical & Electronics Engineering
sections.blurb: Circuits, signals, and the electrical systems that carry and process them.

entries.note: Rate of oscillation of a periodic signal, measured in hertz, central to circuit design and signal processing.

sections.name: Energy Engineering
sections.blurb: Systems for generating and converting power, including renewable energy technology.

entries.note: Devices converting kinetic wind energy into electricity via rotating blades connected to a generator.
